system.name: System 600
system.description: A 600 mm grid-based building system for industrialised housing delivery.

interfaces.name: Wall panel to floor cassette interface

rules.name: Components shall coordinate with the 600 mm system grid
rules.description: Primary component dimensions shall coordinate with the 600 mm System 600 grid.

rules.name: Wall panels shall use standard System 600 widths
rules.description: Wall panels shall use the standard System 600 wall widths identified in the component concepts.

rules.name: Exterior bay assemblies should use standardised dimensions
rules.description: Exterior facade and window assemblies should use standardised bay dimensions derived from the System 600 standardised size diagrams.

rules.name: System should prioritise repeatable element and subsystem granularity
rules.description: System 600 should prioritise repeatable components around element, sub-element, and selected subsystem scale, rather than relying primarily on whole-building or whole-dwelling volumetric modules.

rules.name: Flat and panelised components preferred over full volumetric modules
rules.description: System 600 should prioritise flat and panelised components over full volumetric modularisation, except where pods or service modules provide strategic system value.

rules.name: Decouple complex engineering from architectural flexibility
rules.description: System 600 should decouple standardised performance and engineering layers from flexible architectural and aesthetic layers.

rules.name: Wall finish decoupled from wall frame
rules.description: Exterior wall finish should be decoupled from the wall frame to allow architectural variation without redesigning the underlying structural or performance system.

rules.name: Facade uses multi-layer unitised logic
rules.description: Exterior systems should use a multi-layer unitised logic, separating universal backplane, performance layer, connector or rail, and architectural feature layer.

rules.name: Standardised glazing and weatherproofing
rules.description: Facade components should use standardised glazing units and consistent weatherproofing details.

rules.name: Facade layers use continuous fixing rail
rules.description: Facade architectural and performance layers should connect through a standardised continuous fixing rail or equivalent connector system.

rules.name: Module width must use permitted System 600 width
Module must only reference defined compatible interfaces
Combined lifting point capacity must exceed module mass with safety factor
Services shall be packaged into dedicated modules and racks
rules.description: Services should be packaged into defined components, including an external services module, vertical riser rack, and horizontal distribution rack.

rules.name: External Services Module contains all services except sewer
rules.description: The External Services Module should accommodate riser and service functions for all services except sewer.

rules.name: Vertical riser rack located outside behind ESM
rules.description: Vertical service racks should be located outside the dwelling, behind the External Services Module.

rules.name: Horizontal service rack connects to facade services
rules.description: Horizontal service racks should connect to the facade or external service zone for air intake, exhaust, and refrigerant lines.

rules.name: Horizontal service rack distributes lighting sprinklers and power
rules.description: Horizontal service racks should distribute lighting, sprinklers, and power.

rules.name: Kitchen and bathroom should share wet services
rules.description: Kitchen and bathroom wet services should be co-located or closely coordinated to allow shared sewer and hot/cold water supply routes.

rules.name: Reduce risers overhead work and fire-cell penetrations
rules.description: Service layouts should minimise riser count, overhead service work, and fire-cell penetrations.

rules.name: Horizontal service rack brackets at 600 mm centres
rules.description: Framed brackets for the horizontal service rack shall be preassembled and mounted at every 600 mm to the slab and wall.

rules.name: Horizontal service rack requires demountable finish panel
rules.description: Horizontal service racks should use demountable interior finish panels to support access, serviceability, and installation.

rules.name: External Services Module shall use modular chassis
rules.description: The External Services Module should use a modular chassis that can be lifted as a single assembly or broken down into two cabinets for removal without crane via elevator.

rules.name: External Services Module includes pre-terminated wiring
rules.description: The External Services Module should include pre-terminated wiring loom provision for power and lighting, transported within the ESM.

rules.name: System shall operate as an open curated system
rules.description: System 600 should avoid proprietary lock-in while allowing third-party components and suppliers to participate through shared rules, principles, requirements, and a licensing framework.

source.basis: Open curated system; avoids proprietary lock-in; integration managed by rules and principles.

rules.name: Component integration shall be governed by system rules
rules.description: Components shall only be integrated into System 600 where they satisfy relevant system principles, design rules, component requirements, and schema.

rules.name: Target at least 80 percent standardisation
rules.description: System configurations should minimise unique parts and maximise use of limited SKUs, with a target of at least 80 percent standardisation.

rules.name: Components require fixed flexible free classification
rules.description: Each component shall be classified as fixed, flexible, or free to indicate where design freedom is permitted and where standardisation must be preserved.

rules.name: Components require subsystem classification
rules.description: Every component shall be assigned to a System 600 subsystem.

rules.name: Interfaces shall support interchangeability installation and disassembly
rules.description: Component interfaces should be designed for interchangeability, simplified installation, and disassembly.

rules.name: Component data shall support appropriate fidelity levels
rules.description: Component data should be available at appropriate fidelity levels for design, procurement, logistics, fabrication, and installation.

evidence.title: Wall-to-floor interface prototype
purpose: test lifting point position and accessibility

findings.description: Slotted bracket provides adequate lateral tolerance under mock installation conditions.
findings.implication: Retain slotted connection strategy but verify with structural engineer.

findings.description: Bolt access may become restricted after internal lining is installed.
findings.implication: Revise assembly sequence or bracket access geometry.
